results.title: Suffering
results.description: Unpleasantness, discontentment, dissatisfaction, stress, pain, disease, i.e. mild or intense suffering that is inherent in conditioned existence.

results.title: Mahādukkhakkhandha sutta - The Greater Discourse on the Mass of Suffering
results.description: The Buddha explains how to completely comprehend the gratification, drawback, and escape in the case of sensual pleasures, form, and feeling.

results.title: Dukkha sutta - Suffering
results.description: Six qualities to dwell in ease, without distress, without anguish - 1) thoughts of relinquishment, 2) non-ill will, 3) non-harming, 4) perceptions of relinquishment, 5) non-ill will, 6) non-harming.
results.contentSnippet: “Bhikkhus, endowed with six qualities, a bhikkhu dwells in <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">suffering</mark>, with distress, anguish, and burning i

results.title: Āpāyika sutta - Destined for Suffering
results.description: The Buddha describes the two behaviors that lead to suffering and rebirth in hell if not abandoned.

results.title: Compassion
results.description: A mental quality of wise empathy in response to suffering, which counters qualities of harm or cruelty.
synonyms: kindness towards those who are suffering

results.title: Perceiving escape
results.description: The contemplative perception that recognizes the way out of bondage after understanding gratification and danger, seeing cessation of an unsatisfactory experience as its true escape.
synonyms: recognizing the way leading beyond suffering

results.contentSnippet: In the same way, bhikkhus, when one dwells perceiving gratification in objects that can be grasped at, craving increases. Dependent on craving, clinging arises; dependent on clinging, existence arises

results.title: Abhinandana sutta - Delight
results.description: The Buddha explains that whoever delights in the five aggregates, delights in suffering and is not freed from suffering.
